rules.threshold: Hard flag >100 words; soft flag >75 words
Flag above 6th grade; target 3rd–5th grade = 67% more replies
Exactly one per email; flag any email with >1 ask
Flag if >40% of sentences start with "I" or "We"
Flag any email opening with sender's name, company, or role
Flag any "personalized" element not connected to a business challenge
Flag any follow-up that doesn't introduce new angle, data, or resource
Flag vague claims ("hundreds of companies"); require named company + specific metric
Flag single-block paragraphs >2 sentences; require whitespace

fm1_feature_led_opening.data_signal: Reply rate low, open rate acceptable (email opened but ignored)
fm1_feature_led_opening.copy_signal: Opening sentence subject is "We" or "Our"; bullet points of features/capabilities; buzzword clusters ("AI-powered," "scalable," "platform," "cutting-edge"); fixed pitch block after personalized opener
fm1_feature_led_opening.rewrite_direction: Problem Prompter — open with observation about prospect's world → peer company faced X → status quo → new perspective → soft CTA. Replace every buzzword with a specific quantified outcome.

fm2_fake_personalization.data_signal: Reply rate far below personalization benchmark (genuine personalization = 50–250% lift; fake = zero lift); campaign size >1,000 recipients with <3% reply
fm2_fake_personalization.copy_signal: Compliment disconnected from pitch ("Congrats on the funding!"); vague observations applicable to anyone; {{first_name}}/{{company}} as only personalization; no observable connection between personal opener and the problem being raised
fm2_fake_personalization.rewrite_direction: Observation → Challenge framework. Every personalized element must connect to a likely business challenge. One strong specific signal beats three vague ones. Sources: LinkedIn activity, hiring patterns, BuiltWith, G2 reviews, CEO commentary.

fm3_wrong_persona_targeting.data_signal: All KPIs degrade simultaneously; open rate <15%; A/B testing different copy yields uniformly poor results
fm3_wrong_persona_targeting.copy_signal: Pain points belong to a different role; technical jargon to business buyer or ROI language to engineer; pitch could be sent unchanged to any title at the company; problems the recipient doesn't own
fm3_wrong_persona_targeting.rewrite_direction: Rebuild from persona profile — recalibrate pain language, tone, seniority register, and CTA to the actual recipient's role. Apply the gut check: "Could this message be sent to 10,000 other people? If yes, rewrite it."

fm4_cta_overload.data_signal: Low meeting-book rate even from positive replies; confused or non-committal responses
fm4_cta_overload.copy_signal: More than one question in the email; multiple links; "or" constructions presenting choices; asks that require more than one decision
fm4_cta_overload.rewrite_direction: Compress to single softest-appropriate CTA for the sequence stage. First touch: interest check question. Follow-up 2+: soft ask. Late stage: direct meeting ask. Remove all secondary links and questions.

fm5_too_long_too_dense.data_signal: Reply rate well below benchmark; high mobile non-response rate
fm5_too_long_too_dense.copy_signal: Word count >100; single-block paragraphs; sentence complexity above 6th grade; information density requires active reading rather than scanning
fm5_too_long_too_dense.rewrite_direction: Compress to 25–75 words for first touch. Break into 2–3 paragraphs of 1–2 sentences each. Cut every sentence that doesn't advance the one message. Rewrite for 5th grade reading level.

fm6_too_vague_too_safe.data_signal: Open rate fine; reply rate near zero; no emotional or intellectual reaction from prospect
fm6_too_vague_too_safe.copy_signal: No specific claim made; no tension or challenge to current thinking; language applicable to any company in any industry; avoids any assertion that could be wrong or disputed
fm6_too_vague_too_safe.rewrite_direction: Inject a Challenger-style commercial insight — a specific data point, a counterintuitive observation, or a peer benchmark that creates constructive tension. Something specific enough to be wrong for some readers is specific enough to be right for others.

fm7_methodology_mismatch.data_signal: Reply rate poor despite copy quality; pattern visible when persona × methodology combination is analyzed
fm7_methodology_mismatch.copy_signal: Challenger reframe directed at risk-averse CFO in cost-cutting mode; Sandler negative reverse sent to an IC who needs hand-holding; aggressive urgency tactics sent to an empathy-driven HR buyer; fear-based security messaging (FUD) sent to a sophisticated CISO
fm7_methodology_mismatch.rewrite_direction: Apply the methodology decision tree (Part 3) to select the correct framework for the persona × deal complexity × sequence stage combination. Rebuild structure accordingly.

fm8_buying_stage_mismatch.data_signal: Reply rate poor even with relevant personas; meeting book rate low from positive replies
fm8_buying_stage_mismatch.copy_signal: Solution-stage messaging (demo request, pricing, features) directed at problem-unaware prospects; same message regardless of prior engagement history; no adaptation to buyer's awareness level
fm8_buying_stage_mismatch.rewrite_direction: For unaware buyers: insight-led, create a knowledge gap. For problem-aware: validation and cost-of-inaction framing. For solution-aware: differentiation and social proof. Recalibrate CTA to match awareness level.

fm9_breakup_without_tension.data_signal: Final-step reply rate near zero; no psychological consequence to ignoring
fm9_breakup_without_tension.copy_signal: No real consequence stated; soft language ("I'll stop reaching out"); no call to action that requires a decision; tone indistinguishable from prior follow-ups
fm9_breakup_without_tension.rewrite_direction: Apply Sandler "going for the no" — explicitly state this is the last outreach, give permission to say no, create a genuine decision point. Best performing breakup structure: acknowledge lack of response → assume it's not a priority → explicitly close the file → leave door open without begging.

fm10_subject_body_misalignment.data_signal: High open rate but very low reply rate (subject got click; body lost them immediately)
fm10_subject_body_misalignment.copy_signal: Subject line creates expectations body doesn't fulfill; clickbait subject with consultative body; salesy subject with problem-led body
fm10_subject_body_misalignment.rewrite_direction: Decision — if body is strong, rewrite subject to match using Lavender's "to-do list item" / "internal camouflage" format. If subject got strong opens, rewrite body to deliver on the subject's implied promise.

stage_modifiers.first_touch: Lead with the insight or problem hypothesis. Never pitch the product. CTA = interest check only.
stage_modifiers.follow_up_early: New angle, new evidence. Apply Sandler negative reverse if no response. Begin layering social proof.
stage_modifiers.follow_up_late: Rational drowning (cost of inaction). Peer benchmarks. Sharper CTA.
stage_modifiers.breakup: Sandler "going for the no." Genuine consequence. Last door open without begging.

problem_flip.name: Problem Flip
problem_flip.structure: Rewrite to open with prospect's observable problem, not seller's product

compression.name: Compression
compression.structure: Cut to 25–75 words; 1–2 sentence paragraphs; 5th grade reading level

challenger_reframe.name: Challenger Reframe
challenger_reframe.when_to_use: No insight, no tension, vague messaging
challenger_reframe.structure: Insert commercial teaching insight — specific data point that challenges current thinking

sandler_reverse.name: Sandler Reverse
sandler_reverse.when_to_use: Follow-ups with no response; breakup step
sandler_reverse.structure: Apply negative reverse framing; "going for the no"; permission to disengage

persona_recalibration.name: Persona Recalibration
persona_recalibration.structure: Rebuild pain language, tone, and CTA for actual persona tier

cta_simplification.name: CTA Simplification
cta_simplification.structure: Reduce to single softest-appropriate CTA for stage

social_proof_injection.name: Social Proof Injection
social_proof_injection.when_to_use: Vague claims, skeptical persona, no credibility markers
social_proof_injection.structure: Add named company + specific metric + relevant to prospect's context

stage_realignment.name: Stage Realignment
stage_realignment.structure: Shift to insight-led (unaware), validation (problem-aware), or differentiation (solution-aware)

subject_alignment.name: Subject Alignment
subject_alignment.structure: Rewrite subject to match body using "internal camouflage" format OR rewrite body to deliver on subject promise

personalization_repair.name: Personalization Repair
personalization_repair.structure: Reconnect observation to business challenge via Observation → Challenge framework

breakup_sharpening.name: Breakup Sharpening
breakup_sharpening.structure: Apply Sandler breakup structure — state consequence, give permission to say no, close file

methodology_swap.name: Methodology Swap
methodology_swap.structure: Rebuild structure per decision tree selection

vertical_pain_injection.name: Vertical Pain Injection
vertical_pain_injection.when_to_use: Generic pain language not specific to industry
vertical_pain_injection.structure: Replace with vertical-specific pain language, compliance references, or industry benchmarks

timing_pivot.name: Timing Pivot
timing_pivot.structure: Reframe angle, urgency, and hook for buyer's active timing context (EoQ, new exec, post-funding, etc.)

followup_fresh_angle.name: Follow-up Fresh Angle
followup_fresh_angle.structure: Replace with new data point, case study, or problem angle; new subject line; no reference to prior bump

ceo_founder.title: CEO / Founder (SMB/Mid-market)
ceo_founder.what_works: Challenger (insight-led), referral path (Predictable Revenue); CTA: Soft question, never calendar link on first touch
ceo_founder.what_doesnt: Feature lists, ROI calculators, anything that reads like a vendor pitch

cfo_finance.title: CFO / Finance Buyer
cfo_finance.tone: Formal, data-driven, numbers in subject line work
cfo_finance.what_works: Science of Selling, Value Selling; CTA: Specific ROI conversation ask

cro_vp_sales.title: CRO / VP Sales
cro_vp_sales.what_works: Challenger (peer benchmarking), Gap Selling; CTA: Hard ask acceptable after social proof established
cro_vp_sales.what_doesnt: Long explanations, feature walkthroughs, academic framing

ciso_security.title: CISO / Security Buyer
ciso_security.what_works: MEDDPICC + Challenger; CTA: Resource offer, not meeting request on first touch
ciso_security.what_doesnt: "Next-gen," "AI-powered," cold calls, FUD, generic "security posture" language

cmo_vp_marketing.title: CMO / VP Marketing
cmo_vp_marketing.what_works: Challenger, Gap Selling; CTA: Strategic conversation, not product demo

cto_vp_engineering.title: CTO / VP Engineering
cto_vp_engineering.what_works: Content-led, SNAP; CTA: Technical resource offer, architectural conversation
cto_vp_engineering.what_doesnt: Business jargon, ROI-first framing, HTML emails

vp_director.title: VP / Director (any function)
vp_director.what_works: Challenger, Value Selling; CTA: Peer benchmark conversation, low-commitment ask

revops_gtm_ops.title: RevOps / GTM Ops
revops_gtm_ops.what_works: Value Selling + MEDDPICC; CTA: Operational impact conversation, free audit/assessment
revops_gtm_ops.what_doesnt: Sales-y language; they recognize and reject it immediately

manager_team_lead.title: Manager / Team Lead
manager_team_lead.what_works: Gap Selling, SPIN; CTA: Problem-validation question

hr_people_ops.title: HR / People Ops
hr_people_ops.what_works: Consultative / SPIN; CTA: Resource offer, free playbook, non-committal
hr_people_ops.what_doesnt: Aggressive urgency, cost-only framing, "I noticed you're hiring" without insight tie

individual_contributor.title: Individual Contributor / End User
individual_contributor.what_works: SNAP (Simple, iNvaluable, Aligned, Priority); CTA: Free resource, not a meeting

procurement_legal.title: Procurement / Legal
procurement_legal.what_works: Consultative transparency; CTA: Offer to send compliance docs, pricing comparison, SOC 2 report
procurement_legal.what_doesnt: Cold calls (do not call), manipulation tactics, repeated follow-ups to no-reply, "Seller 101" moves

security_saas.name: Security SaaS
security_saas.language_that_works: Alert fatigue, false positives, MTTD/MTTR, SOC burnout, attack surface expansion, compliance deadlines (SOC 2, NIS2, DORA, PCI DSS, HIPAA), analyst hours wasted, breach cost quantification

devtools.name: DevTools
devtools.language_that_works: Developer velocity, deployment frequency, incident MTTR, on-call burden, tech debt, build times, context switching, DX scores
devtools.avoid: ROI-first framing, business jargon, HTML emails, cold calls to engineers

fintech.name: FinTech
fintech.language_that_works: Regulatory compliance (SOX, PCI, Basel III, GDPR), fraud detection, transaction speed, reconciliation overhead, audit trail, chargeback rates
fintech.avoid: Buzzwords, vague innovation claims, non-specific ROI language

hr_tech.name: HR Tech
hr_tech.language_that_works: Time-to-hire, quality of hire, turnover cost ($100K+ per sales rep), onboarding completion, compliance deadlines, DEI metrics, Glassdoor score, employee NPS
hr_tech.avoid: Cost-only framing, aggressive urgency, treating HR as transactional

revops_sales_tech.name: RevOps / Sales Tech
revops_sales_tech.language_that_works: Lead-to-account matching, routing logic, pipeline accuracy, forecast variance, rep ramp time, attribution gaps, CRM data quality, tool consolidation
revops_sales_tech.avoid: Sales-y language; this persona recognizes and rejects it immediately

end_of_fiscal_quarter.angle_that_works: "Use remaining budget before it expires" · end-of-quarter ROI framing

new_fiscal_year.buyer_state: Planning mode; receptive to new tools
new_fiscal_year.avoid: Rushing to close — they have time

post_funding_series_a_b_c.angle_that_works: Reference growth challenges: "Teams scaling from X to Y typically hit [problem]"

post_layoff_cost_cutting.angle_that_works: Efficiency, consolidation, cost reduction, doing more with less

post_acquisition.avoid: Challenging assumptions about their new direction

new_executive_hire.angle_that_works: "In your first 90 days, most [role] leaders prioritize [X]"
new_executive_hire.avoid: Assuming they know the existing stack

product_launch.avoid: Making it about you; generic congratulations

conference_season_pre.angle_that_works: "Making the most of limited time at [conference]"

conference_season_post.buyer_state: Buried in follow-up; sorting signal from noise
conference_season_post.angle_that_works: Hyper-specific personal detail from actual conversation
conference_season_post.avoid: Badge-scan blast; generic "great to meet you"
conference_season_post.cta_adjustment: Send within 24–48 hours; reference specific detail

summer_slowdown.angle_that_works: Q4 preparation framing; casual tone; lighter emails

end_of_calendar_year.buyer_state: Compressed timeline; high intent when reply
end_of_calendar_year.angle_that_works: "Sign now, implement in January" · budget expiry · micro-calls only

part_8_sequence_architecture.channel_pattern: Email → LinkedIn connection → Email → Phone → LinkedIn message → Email → Phone → Email (breakup)
part_8_sequence_architecture.day_spacing: Steps 1–3: every 2 days. Steps 4–6: every 3 days. Steps 7+: every 4–5 days. Summer: extend all spacing by 1–2 days.
part_8_sequence_architecture.reply_distribution: 93% of all replies arrive by Day 10. First follow-up adds up to 49% more replies over first touch alone. Email steps beyond 5–6 produce sharply declining returns; extend via phone and LinkedIn, not more email.
part_8_sequence_architecture.multichannel_multiplier: Email + LinkedIn + Phone = 287% higher engagement than email-only. Initiating with a cold call doubles email reply rate. Leaving a voicemail doubles likelihood of email reply.

cta_by_stage.example: "Is this on your radar for this quarter?"
"Curious if you're seeing the same challenge."
"Happy to share what [Peer] did — open to it?"
"Should I assume this isn't a priority?"
"I'll close your file — feel free to reach back if the timing changes."

relative_triggers: Step performing >40% below sequence average → flag for rewrite
Step performing >60% below top performer equivalent → flag as critical
Follow-up reply rate higher than first touch → first touch is the problem
Open rate fine, reply rate low → body copy problem (not subject line)
Open rate low, reply rate acceptable from opens → subject line problem

tier_1_required: Step copy (current email or script text)
Sequence stage — first touch, follow-up N, or breakup
Performance data — open rate and reply rate for this step
tier_2_high_value: Deal type — SMB, mid-market, or enterprise
Top performer variant for same step (if available)
tier_3_contextual: Buyer calendar context — timing trigger if known
Prior steps in sequence — what angles have already been used
Persona-specific pain language preferences by vertical
minimum_viable_output_structure: **Diagnosis** — which failure mode(s) detected and why (data signal + copy signal)
**Methodology selection** — which framework governs the rewrite and why
**Rewrite direction(s) selected** — from the taxonomy in Part 4
**Rewritten copy** — applying all invariant rules + methodology + persona calibration
**What changed and why** — diff-style explanation of every major change made
